# interceptor

```go
import "github.com/pion/interceptor"
```

Package interceptor contains the Interceptor interface, with some useful interceptors that should be safe to use in most cases.

## type [Chain](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/chain.go#L7-L9>)

Chain is an interceptor that runs all child interceptors in order.

```go

    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```


<a name="Chain.BindLocalStream"></a>

### func \(\*Chain\) [BindLocalStream](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/chain.go#L38>)

```go
func (i *Chain) BindLocalStream(ctx *StreamInfo, writer RTPWriter) RTPWriter
```

BindLocalStream lets you modify any outgoing RTP packets. It is called once for per LocalStream. The returned method will be called once per rtp packet.

<a name="Chain.BindRTCPReader"></a>

```go
func (i *Chain) BindRTCPReader(reader RTCPReader) RTCPReader
```

BindRTCPReader lets you modify any incoming RTCP packets. It is called once per sender/receiver, however this might change in the future. The returned method will be called once per packet batch.


BindRTCPWriter lets you modify any outgoing RTCP packets. It is called once per PeerConnection. The returned method will be called once per packet batch.

<a name="Interceptor"></a>

## type [Interceptor](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/interceptor.go#L22-L47>)

Interceptor can be used to add functionality to you PeerConnections by modifying any incoming/outgoing rtp/rtcp packets, or sending your own packets as needed.

```go
type Interceptor interface {
    // BindRTCPReader lets you modify any incoming RTCP packets. It is called once per sender/receiver, however this might
    // change in the future. The returned method will be called once per packet batch.
    BindRTCPReader(reader RTCPReader) RTCPReader

    // BindRTCPWriter lets you modify any outgoing RTCP packets. It is called once per PeerConnection. The returned method
    // will be called once per packet batch.
    BindRTCPWriter(writer RTCPWriter) RTCPWriter

    // BindLocalStream lets you modify any outgoing RTP packets. It is called once for per LocalStream. The returned method
    // will be called once per rtp packet.
    BindLocalStream(info *StreamInfo, writer RTPWriter) RTPWriter

    // UnbindLocalStream is called when the Stream is removed. It can be used to clean up any data related to that track.
    UnbindLocalStream(info *StreamInfo)

    // BindRemoteStream lets you modify any incoming RTP packets.
    // It is called once for per RemoteStream. The returned method
    // will be called once per rtp packet.
    BindRemoteStream(info *StreamInfo, reader RTPReader) RTPReader

    // UnbindRemoteStream is called when the Stream is removed. It can be used to clean up any data related to that track.
    UnbindRemoteStream(info *StreamInfo)

    io.Closer
}
```

## type [NoOp](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/noop.go#L8>)

NoOp is an Interceptor that does not modify any packets. It can embedded in other interceptors, so it's possible to implement only a subset of the methods.

```go
type NoOp struct{}
```

<a name="RTCPFeedback"></a>

## type [RTCPFeedback](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/streaminfo.go#L32-L41>)

RTCPFeedback signals the connection to use additional RTCP packet types. https://draft.ortc.org/#dom-rtcrtcpfeedback

```go
type RTCPFeedback struct {
    // Type is the type of feedback.
    // see: https://draft.ortc.org/#dom-rtcrtcpfeedback
    // valid: ack, ccm, nack, goog-remb, transport-cc
    Type string

    // The parameter value depends on the type.
    // For example, type="nack" parameter="pli" will send Picture Loss Indicator packets.
    Parameter string
}
```

<a name="StreamInfo"></a>

## type [StreamInfo](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/streaminfo.go#L13-L28>)

StreamInfo is the Context passed when a StreamLocal or StreamRemote has been Binded or Unbinded.

```go
type StreamInfo struct {
    ID                                string
    Attributes                        Attributes
    SSRC                              uint32
    SSRCRetransmission                uint32
    SSRCForwardErrorCorrection        uint32
    PayloadType                       uint8
    PayloadTypeRetransmission         uint8
    PayloadTypeForwardErrorCorrection uint8
    RTPHeaderExtensions               []RTPHeaderExtension
    MimeType                          string
    ClockRate                         uint32
    Channels                          uint16
    SDPFmtpLine                       string
    RTCPFeedback                      []RTCPFeedback
}
```

<a name="Acknowledgment"></a>

## type [Acknowledgment](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/internal/cc/acknowledgment.go#L15-L22>)

Acknowledgment holds information about a packet and if/when it has been sent/received.

```go
type Acknowledgment struct {
    SequenceNumber uint16 // Either RTP SequenceNumber or TWCC
    SSRC           uint32
    Size           int
    Departure      time.Time
    Arrival        time.Time
    ECN            rtcp.ECN
}
```


<a name="FeedbackAdapter"></a>

## type [FeedbackAdapter](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/internal/cc/feedback_adapter.go#L29-L32>)

FeedbackAdapter converts incoming RTCP Packets \(TWCC and RFC8888\) into Acknowledgments. Acknowledgments are the common format that Congestion Controllers in Pion understand.

```go
type FeedbackAdapter struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewFeedbackAdapter"></a>


```go
func (f *FeedbackAdapter) OnRFC8888Feedback(_ time.Time, feedback *rtcp.CCFeedbackReport) []Acknowledgment
```

OnRFC8888Feedback converts incoming Congestion Control Feedback RTCP packet to Acknowledgments.

```go
func (f *FeedbackAdapter) OnSent(ts time.Time, header *rtp.Header, size int, attributes interceptor.Attributes) error
```

OnSent records that and when an outgoing packet was sent for later mapping to acknowledgments.

<a name="ToTime32"></a>

## func [ToTime32](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/internal/ntp/ntp.go#L42>)

```go
func ToTime32(t uint32, reference time.Time) time.Time
```

ToTime32 converts a uint32 NTP timestamp to a time.Time object, using the highest 16 bit of the reference to recover the lost bits. The low 16 bits are not recovered.

<a name="PacketFactory"></a>

## type [PacketFactory](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/internal/rtpbuffer/packet_factory.go#L18-L20>)

PacketFactory allows custom logic around the handle of RTP Packets before they added to the RTPBuffer. The NoOpPacketFactory doesn't copy packets, while the RetainablePacket will take a copy before adding.

```go
type PacketFactory interface {
    NewPacket(header *rtp.Header, payload []byte, rtxSsrc uint32, rtxPayloadType uint8) (*RetainablePacket, error)
}
```

<a name="PacketFactoryCopy"></a>

## type [PacketFactoryCopy](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/internal/rtpbuffer/packet_factory.go#L23-L27>)

PacketFactoryCopy is PacketFactory that takes a copy of packets when added to the RTPBuffer.

```go
type PacketFactoryCopy struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewPacketFactoryCopy"></a>

### func [NewPacketFactoryCopy](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/internal/rtpbuffer/packet_factory.go#L30>)

```go

```

NewPacketFactoryCopy constructs a PacketFactory that takes a copy of packets when added to the RTPBuffer.

```go
func (m *PacketFactoryCopy) NewPacket(header *rtp.Header, payload []byte, rtxSsrc uint32, rtxPayloadType uint8) (*RetainablePacket, error)
```

NewPacket constructs a new RetainablePacket that can be added to the RTPBuffer.

# cc

```go
import "github.com/pion/interceptor/pkg/cc"
```

Package cc implements an interceptor for bandwidth estimation that can be used with different BandwidthEstimators.

<a name="BandwidthEstimator"></a>

## type [BandwidthEstimator](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/cc/interceptor.go#L23-L30>)

BandwidthEstimator is the interface that will be returned by a NewPeerConnectionCallback and can be used to query current bandwidth metrics and add feedback manually.

```go
type BandwidthEstimator interface {
    AddStream(*interceptor.StreamInfo, interceptor.RTPWriter) interceptor.RTPWriter
    WriteRTCP([]rtcp.Packet, interceptor.Attributes) error
    GetTargetBitrate() int
    OnTargetBitrateChange(f func(bitrate int))
    GetStats() map[string]any
    Close() error
}
```

<a name="BandwidthEstimatorFactory"></a>

## type [BandwidthEstimatorFactory](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/cc/interceptor.go#L18>)

BandwidthEstimatorFactory creates new BandwidthEstimators.

```go
type BandwidthEstimatorFactory func() (BandwidthEstimator, error)
```

<a name="Interceptor"></a>

## type [Interceptor](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/cc/interceptor.go#L91-L96>)

Interceptor implements Google Congestion Control.

```go
type Interceptor struct {
    interceptor.NoOp
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Interceptor.BindLocalStream"></a>

### func \(\*Interceptor\) [BindLocalStream](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/cc/interceptor.go#L129-L131>)

```go
func (c *Interceptor) BindLocalStream(info *interceptor.StreamInfo, writer interceptor.RTPWriter) interceptor.RTPWriter
```

BindLocalStream lets you modify any outgoing RTP packets. It is called once for per LocalStream. The returned method will be called once per rtp packet.

<a name="Interceptor.BindRTCPReader"></a>

### func \(\*Interceptor\) [BindRTCPReader](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/cc/interceptor.go#L101>)

```go
func (c *Interceptor) BindRTCPReader(reader interceptor.RTCPReader) interceptor.RTCPReader
```

BindRTCPReader lets you modify any incoming RTCP packets. It is called once per sender/receiver, however this might change in the future. The returned method will be called once per packet batch.

<a name="Interceptor.Close"></a>

### func \(\*Interceptor\) [Close](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/cc/interceptor.go#L136>)

```go
func (c *Interceptor) Close() error
```

Close closes the interceptor and the associated bandwidth estimator.

# flexfec

```go
import "github.com/pion/interceptor/pkg/flexfec"
```

Package flexfec implements FlexFEC to recover missing RTP packets due to packet loss. https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8627

## Constants

<a name="MaxMediaPackets"></a>Maximum number of media packets that can be protected by a single FEC packet. We are not supporting the possibility of having an FEC packet protect multiple SSRC source packets for now. https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8627#section-4.2.2.1

```go
const (
    MaxMediaPackets uint32 = 110
    MaxFecPackets   uint32 = MaxMediaPackets
)
```

<a name="BaseRTPHeaderSize"></a>

```go
const (
    // BaseRTPHeaderSize represents the minium RTP packet header size in bytes.
    BaseRTPHeaderSize = 12
    // BaseFecHeaderSize represents the minium FEC payload's header size including the
    // required first mask.
    BaseFecHeaderSize = 12
)
```

<a name="BaseFec03HeaderSize"></a>

```go
const (
    // BaseFec03HeaderSize represents the minium FEC payload's header size including the
    // required first mask.
    BaseFec03HeaderSize = 20
)
```

<a name="FecInterceptor"></a>

## type [FecInterceptor](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/flexfec/encoder_interceptor.go#L22-L29>)

FecInterceptor implements FlexFec.

```go
type FecInterceptor struct {
    interceptor.NoOp
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="FecInterceptor.BindLocalStream"></a>

### func \(\*FecInterceptor\) [BindLocalStream](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/flexfec/encoder_interceptor.go#L69-L71>)

```go
func (r *FecInterceptor) BindLocalStream(info *interceptor.StreamInfo, writer interceptor.RTPWriter) interceptor.RTPWriter
```

BindLocalStream lets you modify any outgoing RTP packets. It is called once for per LocalStream. The returned method will be called once per rtp packet.

<a name="FecInterceptor.UnbindLocalStream"></a>

### func \(\*FecInterceptor\) [UnbindLocalStream](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/flexfec/encoder_interceptor.go#L60>)

```go
func (r *FecInterceptor) UnbindLocalStream(info *interceptor.StreamInfo)
```

UnbindLocalStream removes the stream state for a specific SSRC.

FlexEncoder is the interface that FecInterceptor uses to encode Fec packets.

```go
}
```

<a name="FlexEncoder03"></a>

## type [FlexEncoder03](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/flexfec/flexfec_encoder_03.go#L33-L38>)

FlexEncoder03 implements the Fec encoding mechanism for the "Flex" variant of FlexFec.

```go
type FlexEncoder03 struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewFlexEncoder03"></a>

### func [NewFlexEncoder03](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/flexfec/flexfec_encoder_03.go#L49>)

```go
func NewFlexEncoder03(payloadType uint8, ssrc uint32) *FlexEncoder03
```

NewFlexEncoder03 creates new FlexFEC\-03 encoder.

<a name="FlexEncoder03.EncodeFec"></a>

### func \(\*FlexEncoder03\) [EncodeFec](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/flexfec/flexfec_encoder_03.go#L59>)

```go
func (flex *FlexEncoder03) EncodeFec(mediaPackets []rtp.Packet, numFecPackets uint32) []rtp.Packet
```

EncodeFec returns a list of generated RTP packets with FEC payloads that protect the specified mediaPackets. This method returns nil in case of missing RTP packets in the mediaPackets array or packets passed out of order.

## type [FlexEncoder20](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/flexfec/flexfec_encoder.go#L34-L39>)

FlexEncoder20 implementation is WIP, contains bugs and no tests. Check out FlexEncoder03.

```go
type FlexEncoder20 struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewFlexEncoder"></a>

### func [NewFlexEncoder](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/flexfec/flexfec_encoder.go#L43>)

```go
func NewFlexEncoder(payloadType uint8, ssrc uint32) *FlexEncoder20
```

NewFlexEncoder returns a new FlexEncoder20. FlexEncoder20 implementation is WIP, contains bugs and no tests. Check out FlexEncoder03.

<a name="FlexEncoder20.EncodeFec"></a>

### func \(\*FlexEncoder20\) [EncodeFec](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/flexfec/flexfec_encoder.go#L54>)

```go
func (flex *FlexEncoder20) EncodeFec(mediaPackets []rtp.Packet, numFecPackets uint32) []rtp.Packet
```

EncodeFec returns a list of generated RTP packets with FEC payloads that protect the specified mediaPackets. This method does not account for missing RTP packets in the mediaPackets array nor does it account for them being passed out of order.

<a name="ProtectionCoverage"></a>

## type [ProtectionCoverage](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/flexfec/flexfec_coverage.go#L21-L29>)

ProtectionCoverage defines the map of RTP packets that individual Fec packets protect.

```go
type ProtectionCoverage struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewCoverage"></a>

### func [NewCoverage](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/flexfec/flexfec_coverage.go#L34>)

```go
func NewCoverage(mediaPackets []rtp.Packet, numFecPackets uint32) *ProtectionCoverage
```

NewCoverage returns a new ProtectionCoverage object. numFecPackets represents the number of Fec packets that we will be generating to cover the list of mediaPackets. This allows us to know how big the underlying map should be.

<a name="ProtectionCoverage.ExtractMask1"></a>

### func \(\*ProtectionCoverage\) [ExtractMask1](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/flexfec/flexfec_coverage.go#L121>)

```go
func (p *ProtectionCoverage) ExtractMask1(fecPacketIndex uint32) uint16
```

ExtractMask1 returns the first section of the bitmask as defined by the FEC header. https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8627#section-4.2.2.1

<a name="ProtectionCoverage.ExtractMask2"></a>

### func \(\*ProtectionCoverage\) [ExtractMask2](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/flexfec/flexfec_coverage.go#L127>)

```go
func (p *ProtectionCoverage) ExtractMask2(fecPacketIndex uint32) uint32
```

ExtractMask2 returns the second section of the bitmask as defined by the FEC header. https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8627#section-4.2.2.1

<a name="ProtectionCoverage.ExtractMask3"></a>

### func \(\*ProtectionCoverage\) [ExtractMask3](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/flexfec/flexfec_coverage.go#L133>)

```go
func (p *ProtectionCoverage) ExtractMask3(fecPacketIndex uint32) uint64
```

ExtractMask3 returns the third section of the bitmask as defined by the FEC header. https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8627#section-4.2.2.1

<a name="ProtectionCoverage.ExtractMask3_03"></a>

### func \(\*ProtectionCoverage\) [ExtractMask3\\\_03](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/flexfec/flexfec_coverage.go#L139>)

```go
func (p *ProtectionCoverage) ExtractMask3_03(fecPacketIndex uint32) uint64
```

ExtractMask3\_03 returns the third section of the bitmask as defined by the FEC header. https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-payload-flexible-fec-scheme-03#section-4.2

<a name="ProtectionCoverage.GetCoveredBy"></a>

### func \(\*ProtectionCoverage\) [GetCoveredBy](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/flexfec/flexfec_coverage.go#L108>)

```go
func (p *ProtectionCoverage) GetCoveredBy(fecPacketIndex uint32) *util.MediaPacketIterator
```

GetCoveredBy returns an iterator over RTP packets that are protected by the specified Fec packet index.

<a name="ProtectionCoverage.UpdateCoverage"></a>

### func \(\*ProtectionCoverage\) [UpdateCoverage](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/flexfec/flexfec_coverage.go#L62>)

```go
func (p *ProtectionCoverage) UpdateCoverage(mediaPackets []rtp.Packet, numFecPackets uint32)
```

UpdateCoverage updates the ProtectionCoverage object with new bitmasks accounting for the numFecPackets we want to use to protect the batch media packets.

# gcc

```go
import "github.com/pion/interceptor/pkg/gcc"
```

Package gcc implements Google Congestion Control for bandwidth estimation

## Variables

<a name="ErrSendSideBWEClosed"></a>ErrSendSideBWEClosed is raised when SendSideBWE.WriteRTCP is called after SendSideBWE.Close.

```go
var ErrSendSideBWEClosed = errors.New("SendSideBwe closed")
```

<a name="ErrUnknownStream"></a>ErrUnknownStream is returned when trying to send a packet with a SSRC that was never registered with any stream.

```go
var ErrUnknownStream = errors.New("unknown ssrc")
```

# intervalpli

```go
import "github.com/pion/interceptor/pkg/intervalpli"
```

Package intervalpli is an interceptor that requests PLI on a static interval. Useful when bridging protocols that don't have receiver feedback.

## type [GeneratorInterceptor](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/intervalpli/generator_interceptor.go#L34-L47>)

GeneratorInterceptor interceptor sends PLI packets. Implements PLI in a naive way: sends a PLI for each new track that support PLI, periodically.

```go
type GeneratorInterceptor struct {
    interceptor.NoOp
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

```go
func (r *GeneratorInterceptor) BindRTCPReader(reader interceptor.RTCPReader) interceptor.RTCPReader
```

BindRTCPReader lets you modify any incoming RTCP packets. It is called once per sender/receiver, however this might change in the future. The returned method will be called once per packet batch.


BindRTCPWriter lets you modify any outgoing RTCP packets. It is called once per PeerConnection. The returned method will be called once per packet batch.

BindRemoteStream lets you modify any incoming RTP packets. It is called once for per RemoteStream. The returned method will be called once per rtp packet.

# jitterbuffer

```go
import "github.com/pion/interceptor/pkg/jitterbuffer"
```

Package jitterbuffer implements a buffer for RTP packets designed to help counteract non\-deterministic sources of latency

## Constants

<a name="StartBuffering"></a>

```go
const (
    // StartBuffering is emitted when the buffer receives its first packet.
    StartBuffering Event = "startBuffering"
    // BeginPlayback is emitted when the buffer has satisfied its buffer length.
    BeginPlayback = "playing"
    // BufferUnderflow is emitted when the buffer does not have enough packets to Pop.
    BufferUnderflow = "underflow"
    // BufferOverflow is emitted when the buffer has exceeded its limit.
    BufferOverflow = "overflow"
)
```

## Variables

<a name="ErrBufferUnderrun"></a>

```go
var (
    // ErrBufferUnderrun is returned when the buffer has no items.
    ErrBufferUnderrun = errors.New("invalid Peek: Empty jitter buffer")
    // ErrPopWhileBuffering is returned if a jitter buffer is not in a playback state.
    ErrPopWhileBuffering = errors.New("attempt to pop while buffering")
)
```

<a name="ErrInvalidOperation"></a>

```go
var (
    // ErrInvalidOperation may be returned if a Pop or Find operation is performed on an empty queue.
    ErrInvalidOperation = errors.New("attempt to find or pop on an empty list")
    // ErrNotFound will be returned if the packet cannot be found in the queue.
    ErrNotFound = errors.New("priority not found")
)
```

<a name="JitterBuffer"></a>

## type [JitterBuffer](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/jitterbuffer/jitter_buffer.go#L68-L79>)

A JitterBuffer will accept Pushed packets, put them in sequence number order, and allows removing in either sequence number order or via a provided timestamp.

```go
type JitterBuffer struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="New"></a>

### func [New](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/jitterbuffer/jitter_buffer.go#L95>)

```go
func New(opts ...Option) *JitterBuffer
```

New will initialize a jitter buffer and its associated statistics.

```go
func (jb *JitterBuffer) Clear(resetState bool)
```

Clear will empty the buffer and optionally reset the state.

<a name="JitterBuffer.Listen"></a>

### func \(\*JitterBuffer\) [Listen](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/jitterbuffer/jitter_buffer.go#L123>)

```go
func (jb *JitterBuffer) Listen(event Event, cb EventListener)
```

Listen will register an event listener The jitter buffer may emit events correspnding, interested listerns should look at Event for available events.

<a name="JitterBuffer.Peek"></a>

### func \(\*JitterBuffer\) [Peek](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/jitterbuffer/jitter_buffer.go#L200>)

```go
func (jb *JitterBuffer) Peek(playoutHead bool) (*rtp.Packet, error)
```

Peek at the packet which is either:

```
At the playout head when we are emitting, and the playoutHead flag is true
```

or else

```
At the last sequence received
```

<a name="JitterBuffer.PeekAtSequence"></a>

### func \(\*JitterBuffer\) [PeekAtSequence](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/jitterbuffer/jitter_buffer.go#L255>)

```go
func (jb *JitterBuffer) PeekAtSequence(sq uint16) (*rtp.Packet, error)
```

PeekAtSequence will return an RTP packet from the jitter buffer at the specified Sequence without removing it from the buffer.

<a name="JitterBuffer.PlayoutHead"></a>

### func \(\*JitterBuffer\) [PlayoutHead](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/jitterbuffer/jitter_buffer.go#L128>)

```go
func (jb *JitterBuffer) PlayoutHead() uint16
```

PlayoutHead returns the SequenceNumber that will be attempted to Pop next.

<a name="JitterBuffer.Pop"></a>

### func \(\*JitterBuffer\) [Pop](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/jitterbuffer/jitter_buffer.go#L214>)

```go
func (jb *JitterBuffer) Pop() (*rtp.Packet, error)
```

Pop an RTP packet from the jitter buffer at the current playout head.

<a name="JitterBuffer.PopAtSequence"></a>

### func \(\*JitterBuffer\) [PopAtSequence](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/jitterbuffer/jitter_buffer.go#L234>)

```go
func (jb *JitterBuffer) PopAtSequence(sq uint16) (*rtp.Packet, error)
```

PopAtSequence will pop an RTP packet from the jitter buffer at the specified Sequence.

<a name="JitterBuffer.PopAtTimestamp"></a>

### func \(\*JitterBuffer\) [PopAtTimestamp](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/jitterbuffer/jitter_buffer.go#L268>)

```go
func (jb *JitterBuffer) PopAtTimestamp(ts uint32) (*rtp.Packet, error)
```

PopAtTimestamp pops an RTP packet from the jitter buffer with the provided timestamp Call this method repeatedly to drain the buffer at the timestamp.

<a name="JitterBuffer.Push"></a>

### func \(\*JitterBuffer\) [Push](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/jitterbuffer/jitter_buffer.go#L156>)

```go
func (jb *JitterBuffer) Push(packet *rtp.Packet)
```

Push an RTP packet into the jitter buffer, this does not clone the data so if the memory is expected to be reused, the caller should take this in to account and pass a copy of the packet they wish to buffer.

<a name="JitterBuffer.SetPlayoutHead"></a>

### func \(\*JitterBuffer\) [SetPlayoutHead](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/jitterbuffer/jitter_buffer.go#L137>)

```go
func (jb *JitterBuffer) SetPlayoutHead(playoutHead uint16)
```

SetPlayoutHead allows you to manually specify the packet you wish to pop next If you have encountered a packet that hasn't resolved you can skip it.

<a name="PriorityQueue"></a>

## type [PriorityQueue](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/jitterbuffer/priority_queue.go#L13-L16>)

PriorityQueue provides a linked list sorting of RTP packets by SequenceNumber.

```go
type PriorityQueue struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewQueue"></a>

### func [NewQueue](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/jitterbuffer/priority_queue.go#L35>)

```go
func NewQueue() *PriorityQueue
```

NewQueue will create a new PriorityQueue whose order relies on monotonically increasing Sequence Number, wrapping at MaxUint16, so a packet with sequence number MaxUint16 \- 1 will be after 0.

Find a packet in the queue with the provided sequence number, regardless of position \(the packet is retained in the queue\).


PopAt removes an element at the specified sequence number \(priority\).


```go
func (q *PriorityQueue) Push(val *rtp.Packet, priority uint16)
```

Push will insert a packet in to the queue in order of sequence number.

<a name="ReceiverInterceptor"></a>

## type [ReceiverInterceptor](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/jitterbuffer/receiver_interceptor.go#L59-L67>)

ReceiverInterceptor places a JitterBuffer in the chain to smooth packet arrival and allow for network jitter

```
The Interceptor is designed to fit in a RemoteStream
pipeline and buffer incoming packets for a short period (currently
defaulting to 50 packets) before emitting packets to be consumed by the
next step in the pipeline.

The caller must ensure they are prepared to handle an
ErrPopWhileBuffering in the case that insufficient packets have been
received by the jitter buffer. The caller should retry the operation
at some point later as the buffer may have been filled in the interim.

The caller should also be aware that an ErrBufferUnderrun may be
returned in the case that the initial buffering was sufficient and
playback began but the caller is consuming packets (or they are not
arriving) quickly enough.
```

```go
type ReceiverInterceptor struct {
    interceptor.NoOp
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="ReceiverInterceptor.BindRemoteStream"></a>

### func \(\*ReceiverInterceptor\) [BindRemoteStream](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/jitterbuffer/receiver_interceptor.go#L76-L78>)

```go
func (i *ReceiverInterceptor) BindRemoteStream(_ *interceptor.StreamInfo, reader interceptor.RTPReader) interceptor.RTPReader
```

BindRemoteStream lets you modify any incoming RTP packets. It is called once for per RemoteStream. The returned method will be called once per rtp packet.

<a name="ReceiverInterceptor.Close"></a>

### func \(\*ReceiverInterceptor\) [Close](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/jitterbuffer/receiver_interceptor.go#L115>)

```go
func (i *ReceiverInterceptor) Close() error
```

Close closes the interceptor.

```go
func (i *ReceiverInterceptor) UnbindRemoteStream(_ *interceptor.StreamInfo)
```

UnbindRemoteStream is called when the Stream is removed. It can be used to clean up any data related to that track.

<a name="State"></a>

## type [State](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/jitterbuffer/jitter_buffer.go#L16>)

State tracks a JitterBuffer as either Buffering or Emitting.

```go
type State uint16
```

<a name="Buffering"></a>

```go
const (
    // Buffering is the state when the jitter buffer has not started emitting yet,
    // or has hit an underflow and needs to re-buffer packets.
    Buffering State = iota
    //  Emitting is the state when the jitter buffer is operating nominally
    Emitting
)
```

<a name="State.String"></a>

### func \(State\) [String](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/jitterbuffer/jitter_buffer.go#L47>)

```go

```

<a name="Stats"></a>

## type [Stats](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/jitterbuffer/jitter_buffer.go#L88-L92>)

Stats Track interesting statistics for the life of this JitterBuffer outOfOrderCount will provide the number of times a packet was Pushed

```
without its predecessor being present
```

underflowCount will provide the count of attempts to Pop an empty buffer overflowCount will track the number of times the jitter buffer exceeds its limit.

```go
type Stats struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

## type [Interceptor](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/mock/interceptor.go#L14-L22>)

Interceptor is an mock Interceptor fot testing.

```go
type Interceptor struct {
    BindRTCPReaderFn     func(reader interceptor.RTCPReader) interceptor.RTCPReader
    BindRTCPWriterFn     func(writer interceptor.RTCPWriter) interceptor.RTCPWriter
    BindLocalStreamFn    func(i *interceptor.StreamInfo, writer interceptor.RTPWriter) interceptor.RTPWriter
    UnbindLocalStreamFn  func(i *interceptor.StreamInfo)
    BindRemoteStreamFn   func(i *interceptor.StreamInfo, reader interceptor.RTPReader) interceptor.RTPReader
    UnbindRemoteStreamFn func(i *interceptor.StreamInfo)
    CloseFn              func() error
}
```

# nack

```go
import "github.com/pion/interceptor/pkg/nack"
```

Package nack provides interceptors to implement sending and receiving negative acknowledgements

<a name="ErrInvalidSize"></a>ErrInvalidSize is returned by newReceiveLog/newRTPBuffer, when an incorrect buffer size is supplied.

```go
var ErrInvalidSize = rtpbuffer.ErrInvalidSize
```

<a name="GeneratorInterceptor"></a>

## type [GeneratorInterceptor](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/nack/generator_interceptor.go#L56-L72>)

GeneratorInterceptor interceptor generates nack feedback messages.

```go
type GeneratorInterceptor struct {
    interceptor.NoOp
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="GeneratorInterceptor.BindRTCPWriter"></a>

### func \(\*GeneratorInterceptor\) [BindRTCPWriter](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/nack/generator_interceptor.go#L81>)

```go
func (n *GeneratorInterceptor) BindRTCPWriter(writer interceptor.RTCPWriter) interceptor.RTCPWriter
```

BindRTCPWriter lets you modify any outgoing RTCP packets. It is called once per PeerConnection. The returned method will be called once per packet batch.

<a name="GeneratorInterceptor.BindRemoteStream"></a>

### func \(\*GeneratorInterceptor\) [BindRemoteStream](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/nack/generator_interceptor.go#L98-L100>)

```go
func (n *GeneratorInterceptor) BindRemoteStream(info *interceptor.StreamInfo, reader interceptor.RTPReader) interceptor.RTPReader
```

BindRemoteStream lets you modify any incoming RTP packets. It is called once for per RemoteStream. The returned method will be called once per rtp packet.

<a name="GeneratorInterceptor.Close"></a>

### func \(\*GeneratorInterceptor\) [Close](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/nack/generator_interceptor.go#L140>)

```go
func (n *GeneratorInterceptor) Close() error
```

Close closes the interceptor.

<a name="GeneratorInterceptor.UnbindRemoteStream"></a>

### func \(\*GeneratorInterceptor\) [UnbindRemoteStream](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/nack/generator_interceptor.go#L131>)

```go
func (n *GeneratorInterceptor) UnbindRemoteStream(info *interceptor.StreamInfo)
```

UnbindRemoteStream is called when the Stream is removed. It can be used to clean up any data related to that track.

<a name="GeneratorOption"></a>

## type [GeneratorOption](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/nack/generator_option.go#L14>)

GeneratorOption can be used to configure GeneratorInterceptor.

```go
type GeneratorOption func(r *GeneratorInterceptor) error
```

<a name="GeneratorInterval"></a>

### func [GeneratorInterval](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/nack/generator_option.go#L66>)

```go
func GeneratorInterval(interval time.Duration) GeneratorOption
```

GeneratorInterval sets the nack send interval for the interceptor.

<a name="GeneratorLog"></a>

### func [GeneratorLog](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/nack/generator_option.go#L48>)

```go
func GeneratorLog(log logging.LeveledLogger) GeneratorOption
```

GeneratorLog sets a logger for the interceptor.

<a name="GeneratorMaxNacksPerPacket"></a>

### func [GeneratorMaxNacksPerPacket](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/nack/generator_option.go#L39>)

```go
func GeneratorMaxNacksPerPacket(maxNacks uint16) GeneratorOption
```

GeneratorMaxNacksPerPacket sets the maximum number of NACKs sent per missing packet, e.g. if set to 2, a missing packet will only be NACKed at most twice. If set to 0 \(default\), max number of NACKs is unlimited.

<a name="GeneratorSize"></a>

### func [GeneratorSize](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/nack/generator_option.go#L18>)

```go
func GeneratorSize(size uint16) GeneratorOption
```

GeneratorSize sets the size of the interceptor. Size must be one of: 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048, 4096, 8192, 16384, 32768.

<a name="GeneratorSkipLastN"></a>

### func [GeneratorSkipLastN](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/nack/generator_option.go#L29>)

```go
func GeneratorSkipLastN(skipLastN uint16) GeneratorOption
```

GeneratorSkipLastN sets the number of packets \(n\-1 packets before the last received packets\)

```
to ignore when generating nack requests.
```


GeneratorStreamsFilter sets filter for generator streams.

<a name="ResponderInterceptor"></a>

## type [ResponderInterceptor](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/nack/responder_interceptor.go#L53-L63>)

ResponderInterceptor responds to nack feedback messages.

```go
type ResponderInterceptor struct {
    interceptor.NoOp
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="ResponderInterceptor.BindLocalStream"></a>

### func \(\*ResponderInterceptor\) [BindLocalStream](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/nack/responder_interceptor.go#L107-L109>)

```go
func (n *ResponderInterceptor) BindLocalStream(info *interceptor.StreamInfo, writer interceptor.RTPWriter) interceptor.RTPWriter
```

BindLocalStream lets you modify any outgoing RTP packets. It is called once for per LocalStream. The returned method will be called once per rtp packet.

<a name="ResponderInterceptor.BindRTCPReader"></a>

### func \(\*ResponderInterceptor\) [BindRTCPReader](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/nack/responder_interceptor.go#L78>)

```go
func (n *ResponderInterceptor) BindRTCPReader(reader interceptor.RTCPReader) interceptor.RTCPReader
```

BindRTCPReader lets you modify any incoming RTCP packets. It is called once per sender/receiver, however this might change in the future. The returned method will be called once per packet batch.

<a name="ResponderInterceptor.UnbindLocalStream"></a>

### func \(\*ResponderInterceptor\) [UnbindLocalStream](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/nack/responder_interceptor.go#L146>)

```go
func (n *ResponderInterceptor) UnbindLocalStream(info *interceptor.StreamInfo)
```

UnbindLocalStream is called when the Stream is removed. It can be used to clean up any data related to that track.

<a name="DisableCopy"></a>

### func [DisableCopy](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/nack/responder_option.go#L45>)

```go
func DisableCopy() ResponderOption
```

DisableCopy bypasses copy of underlying packets. It should be used when you are not re\-using underlying buffers of packets that have been written.

```go
type PacketLogger interface {

}
```

<a name="RTCPBinaryFormatCallback"></a>

## type [RTCPBinaryFormatCallback](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/packetdump/format.go#L46>)

RTCPBinaryFormatCallback can be used to apply custom formatting or marshaling to each dumped RTCP packet.

```go
type RTCPBinaryFormatCallback func(rtcp.Packet, interceptor.Attributes) ([]byte, error)
```

<a name="RTCPFilterCallback"></a>

## type [RTCPFilterCallback](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/packetdump/filter.go#L19>)

RTCPFilterCallback can be used to filter RTCP packets to dump. The callback returns whether or not to print dump the packet's content.

Deprecated: prefer RTCPPerPacketFilterCallback.

```go
type RTCPFilterCallback func(pkt []rtcp.Packet) bool
```

<a name="RTCPFormatCallback"></a>

## type [RTCPFormatCallback](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/packetdump/format.go#L26>)

RTCPFormatCallback can be used to apply custom formatting to each dumped RTCP packet. If new lines should be added after each packet, they must be included in the returned format.

Deprecated: prefer RTCPBinaryFormatCallback.

```go
type RTCPFormatCallback func([]rtcp.Packet, interceptor.Attributes) string
```

<a name="RTCPPerPacketFilterCallback"></a>

## type [RTCPPerPacketFilterCallback](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/packetdump/filter.go#L23>)

RTCPPerPacketFilterCallback can be used to filter RTCP packets to dump. It's called once per every packet opposing to RTCPFilterCallback which is called once per packet batch.

```go
type RTCPPerPacketFilterCallback func(pkt rtcp.Packet) bool
```

<a name="RTPBinaryFormatCallback"></a>

## type [RTPBinaryFormatCallback](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/packetdump/format.go#L43>)

RTPBinaryFormatCallback can be used to apply custom formatting or marshaling to each dumped RTP packet.

```go
type RTPBinaryFormatCallback func(*rtp.Packet, interceptor.Attributes) ([]byte, error)
```

<a name="RTPFilterCallback"></a>

## type [RTPFilterCallback](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/packetdump/filter.go#L13>)

RTPFilterCallback can be used to filter RTP packets to dump. The callback returns whether or not to print dump the packet's content.

```go
type RTPFilterCallback func(pkt *rtp.Packet) bool
```

<a name="RTPFormatCallback"></a>

## type [RTPFormatCallback](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/packetdump/format.go#L19>)

RTPFormatCallback can be used to apply custom formatting to each dumped RTP packet. If new lines should be added after each packet, they must be included in the returned format.

Deprecated: prefer RTPBinaryFormatCallback.

```go

```

<a name="ReceiverInterceptor"></a>

## type [ReceiverInterceptor](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/packetdump/receiver_interceptor.go#L37-L40>)

ReceiverInterceptor interceptor dumps outgoing RTP packets.

```go
type ReceiverInterceptor struct {
    interceptor.NoOp
    *PacketDumper
}
```

<a name="ReceiverInterceptor.BindRTCPReader"></a>

### func \(\*ReceiverInterceptor\) [BindRTCPReader](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/packetdump/receiver_interceptor.go#L71>)

```go
func (r *ReceiverInterceptor) BindRTCPReader(reader interceptor.RTCPReader) interceptor.RTCPReader
```

BindRTCPReader lets you modify any incoming RTCP packets. It is called once per sender/receiver, however this might change in the future. The returned method will be called once per packet batch.

<a name="ReceiverInterceptor.BindRemoteStream"></a>

```go
func (r *ReceiverInterceptor) BindRemoteStream(_ *interceptor.StreamInfo, reader interceptor.RTPReader) interceptor.RTPReader
```

BindRemoteStream lets you modify any incoming RTP packets. It is called once for per RemoteStream. The returned method will be called once per rtp packet.

```go
func (r *ReceiverInterceptor) Close() error
```

Close closes the interceptor.

# report

```go
import "github.com/pion/interceptor/pkg/report"
```

Package report provides interceptors to implement sending sender and receiver reports.

# rtpfb

```go
import "github.com/pion/interceptor/pkg/rtpfb"
```

Package rtpfb implements feedback aggregation for CCFB and TWCC packets.

## Index

- [type Interceptor](<#Interceptor>)
  - [func \(i \*Interceptor\) BindLocalStream\(info \*interceptor.StreamInfo, writer interceptor.RTPWriter\) interceptor.RTPWriter](<#Interceptor.BindLocalStream>)
  - [func \(i \*Interceptor\) BindRTCPReader\(reader interceptor.RTCPReader\) interceptor.RTCPReader](<#Interceptor.BindRTCPReader>)
- [type InterceptorFactory](<#InterceptorFactory>)
  - [func NewInterceptor\(opts ...Option\) \(\*InterceptorFactory, error\)](<#NewInterceptor>)
  - [func \(f \*InterceptorFactory\) NewInterceptor\(\_ string\) \(interceptor.Interceptor, error\)](<#InterceptorFactory.NewInterceptor>)
- [type Option](<#Option>)
  - [func WithLoggerFactory\(lf logging.LoggerFactory\) Option](<#WithLoggerFactory>)
- [type PacketReport](<#PacketReport>)
- [type Report](<#Report>)

<a name="Interceptor"></a>

## type [Interceptor](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/rtpfb/interceptor.go#L103-L110>)

Interceptor implements a congestion control feedback receiver. It keeps track of outgoing packets and reads incoming feedback reports \(CCFB or TWCC\). For each incoming feedback report, it will add an entry to the interceptor attributes, which can be read from the \`RTCPReader\` \(\`webrtc.RTPSender.Read\`\). For each acknowledgement included in the feedback report, a PacketReport will be added to the ccfb.Report.

```go
type Interceptor struct {
    interceptor.NoOp
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Interceptor.BindLocalStream"></a>

### func \(\*Interceptor\) [BindLocalStream](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/rtpfb/interceptor.go#L167-L170>)

```go

```

BindLocalStream implements interceptor.Interceptor.

<a name="Interceptor.BindRTCPReader"></a>

### func \(\*Interceptor\) [BindRTCPReader](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/rtpfb/interceptor.go#L189>)

```go
func (i *Interceptor) BindRTCPReader(reader interceptor.RTCPReader) interceptor.RTCPReader
```

BindRTCPReader implements interceptor.Interceptor.

InterceptorFactory is a factory for CCFB interceptors.

```go

    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

### func [NewInterceptor](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/rtpfb/interceptor.go#L72>)

```go

```

```go

```

## type [Option](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/rtpfb/interceptor.go#L40>)

Option can be used to set initial options on CCFB interceptors.

```go
type Option func(*Interceptor) error
```

<a name="WithLoggerFactory"></a>

### func [WithLoggerFactory](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/rtpfb/interceptor.go#L42>)

```go
func WithLoggerFactory(lf logging.LoggerFactory) Option
```

<a name="PacketReport"></a>

## type [PacketReport](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/rtpfb/report.go#L12-L52>)

```go
type PacketReport struct {
    // SSRC of the stream the packet was sent on.
    SSRC uint32

    // Sequence number of the packet generated by the rtpfb.Interceptor. The
    // sequence number increases by 1 for every outgoing packet independent of
    // the stream it is sent on.
    SequenceNumber uint64

    // Sequence number from the RTP header.
    RTPSequenceNumber uint16

    // IsTWCC is true if the packet was tracked by the
    // transport-wide-congestion-control header extension instead of the
    // combination of SSRC and RTPSequenceNumber.
    IsTWCC bool

    // Sequence number from the transport-wide-congestion-control header
    // extension.
    TWCCSequenceNumber uint16

    // Size is the size of the packet in bytes.
    Size int

    // Arrived indicates if the packet arrived at the receiver. False does not
    // necessarily mean the packet was lost, it might still be in transit.
    Arrived bool

    // Departure is the departure time of the packet taken at the sender. It
    // should be the time measured at the latest possible moment before sending
    // the packet.
    Departure time.Time

    // Arrival is the arrival time of the packet at the receiver. Arrival and
    // Departure do not require synchronized clocks and can therefore not
    // directly be compared.
    Arrival time.Time

    // ECN marking of the packet when it arrived.
    ECN rtcp.ECN
}
```

<a name="Report"></a>

## type [Report](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/rtpfb/report.go#L58-L62>)

A Report contains the Arrival time of a CCFB or TWCC packet, the estimated RTT based on the feedback packet and a list of PacketReport for all acknowledged packets that were still in the history and not yet included in an earlier Report.

```go
type Report struct {
    Arrival       time.Time
    RTT           time.Duration
    PacketReports []PacketReport
}
```

<a name="Recorder"></a>

## type [Recorder](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/stats/interceptor.go#L110-L118>)

Recorder is the interface of a statistics recorder.

```go
type Recorder interface {
    QueueIncomingRTP(ts time.Time, buf []byte, attr interceptor.Attributes)
    QueueIncomingRTCP(ts time.Time, buf []byte, attr interceptor.Attributes)
    QueueOutgoingRTP(ts time.Time, header *rtp.Header, payload []byte, attr interceptor.Attributes)
    QueueOutgoingRTCP(ts time.Time, pkts []rtcp.Packet, attr interceptor.Attributes)
    GetStats() Stats
    Stop()
    Start()
}
```


## type [HeaderExtensionInterceptor](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/twcc/header_extension_interceptor.go#L30-L33>)

HeaderExtensionInterceptor adds transport wide sequence numbers as header extension to each RTP packet.

```go
    interceptor.NoOp
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

```go
func (h *HeaderExtensionInterceptor) BindLocalStream(info *interceptor.StreamInfo, writer interceptor.RTPWriter) interceptor.RTPWriter
```

<a name="Recorder"></a>

## type [Recorder](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/twcc/twcc.go#L22-L36>)

Recorder records incoming RTP packets and their delays and creates transport wide congestion control feedback reports as specified in https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-holmer-rmcat-transport-wide-cc-extensions-01

```go
type Recorder struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

```go

```

NewRecorder creates a new Recorder which uses the given senderSSRC in the created feedback packets.

```go

```

BuildFeedbackPacket creates a new RTCP packet containing a TWCC feedback report.


PacketsHeld returns the number of received packets currently held by the recorder.

```go
func (r *Recorder) Record(mediaSSRC uint32, sequenceNumber uint16, arrivalTime int64)
```

Record marks a packet with mediaSSRC and a transport wide sequence number sequenceNumber as received at arrivalTime.

<a name="SenderInterceptor"></a>

## type [SenderInterceptor](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/twcc/sender_interceptor.go#L57-L72>)

SenderInterceptor sends transport wide congestion control reports as specified in: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-holmer-rmcat-transport-wide-cc-extensions-01

```go
type SenderInterceptor struct {
    interceptor.NoOp
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="SenderInterceptor.BindRTCPWriter"></a>

### func \(\*SenderInterceptor\) [BindRTCPWriter](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/twcc/sender_interceptor.go#L98>)

```go
func (s *SenderInterceptor) BindRTCPWriter(writer interceptor.RTCPWriter) interceptor.RTCPWriter
```

BindRTCPWriter lets you modify any outgoing RTCP packets. It is called once per PeerConnection. The returned method will be called once per packet batch.

<a name="SenderInterceptor.BindRemoteStream"></a>

### func \(\*SenderInterceptor\) [BindRemoteStream](<https://github.com/pion/interceptor/blob/master/pkg/twcc/sender_interceptor.go#L127-L129>)

```go
func (s *SenderInterceptor) BindRemoteStream(info *interceptor.StreamInfo, reader interceptor.RTPReader) interceptor.RTPReader
```

BindRemoteStream lets you modify any incoming RTP packets. It is called once for per RemoteStream. The returned method will be called once per rtp packet.

```go
func (s *SenderInterceptor) Close() error
```

Close closes the interceptor.
